steps.text: Smoke at 121–135 °C (250–275 °F) until bark is dry and firm (usually 4–5 hr).
Place in an open aluminium foil boat — bottom braises in collected juices.
Bump pit to 149 °C (300 °F); convective heat renders the fat cap on top.
Finish ~95–96 °C (203–205 °F) internal average on the flat.
Au jus: boat drippings + one stick melted butter on slices.
Pull at 90.5 °C (195 °F) — ~40% rendered; flat should feel tight, not butter-tender.
Wrap and go straight into a 65.5 °C (150 °F) holding oven ~18 hours.
~4 hr temperature decline from 90.5 → 65.5 °C finishes much of the remaining ~60% render.
Gentle hold to ~100% without muscle fibres denaturing past 93.3 °C on the pit.
Slice — eats like prime rib more than classic brisket.
Wrap in butcher paper before the brisket hits the smoker.
Next day: smoker at 121 °C (250 °F) until internal reaches ~65.5 °C (150 °F).
Less total smoke than traditional — very juicy.
